div.head: CO–38. Telegram from the Acting Secretary of State to the Embassy in ColombiaSource: Department of State, Central Files, 721.5-MSP/12–160. Secret. Drafted by Frank J. Devine, Special Assistant to the Assistant Secretary of State for Inter-American Affairs, and signed by Mann.
div.p:
Embtel 268Telegram 268 form Bogotá, December 1, 1960, reads as follows:
“There is increasing irritation here at highest government levels at lack U.S. assistance combat violence as requested by President Lleras year and half ago. Important in our interests make immediate decision regarding aid program and implement it quickly.” (721.5–MSP/12–160)
Not yet possible furnish any official assurances GOC re U.S. assistance anti-violence program.

FYI Dept has been actively pushing this matter and necessary Presidential Determination now ready in draft and distributed other
agencies. Dept hopes receive all comments and necessary clearances and be in position request White House approval during coming week. Once latter obtained you will be notified soonest.
